vitals.date: No confirmed edition since 2019 — not currently bookable
vitals.date_specific: Last confirmed edition: July 14, 2019; a planned July 19, 2020 edition was cancelled by Quebec's COVID-19 event shutdown and never revived
vitals.field_size: ~400 riders (2014, best-documented historical figure); the promotional '2,000 riders' claim was never independently verified

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 66. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 61. FLAG (2026-07-24 editorial wave 8): no confirmed edition since 2019 (planned July 2020 edition cancelled by Quebec's COVID-19 event shutdown, never revived) — every dimension score here (organization=4, field_depth=2, etc.) was set against a currently non-bookable event, matching the gran-fondo-mont-tremblant/birkebeinerrittet-road precedent. Recommend a human decide whether this profile should carry an active-style score at all. Numbers left untouched per no-silent-reshuffle rule.

course_description.character: IMPORTANT — no confirmed edition has run since 2019. When it operated, this was an all-road Côte-de-Beaupré ride largely following Avenue Royale/Route de la Nouvelle-France near the St. Lawrence, then turning inland into the Laurentian foothills through L'Ange-Gardien, Château-Richer and Saint-Ferréol-les-Neiges, plus rang Saint-Achillée, Saint-Léon and Saint-Elzéar. 2014 offered four distances: 46, 80, 121 and 161 km. The largest ('Alto', ~160-161 km) advertised roughly 2,550 m of climbing with ramps to 17%. This database's prior 130.4 km/1,800 m figures are geographically plausible for the region but do not match any documented standard event distance — the closest historical routes were 115-121 km (medium) or 160-161 km (long); treat 130.4/1,800 as unverified rather than canonical.
course_description.signature_challenge: Historically, the climb through Saint-Ferréol-les-Neiges and the inland Laurentian foothill roads (rang Saint-Achillée, Saint-Léon, Saint-Elzéar) on the 160 km Alto course — no longer verifiable as current given the event's lapsed status since 2019.
suffering_zones: L'Ange-Gardien and Château-Richer — the historic Côte-de-Beaupré segments closest to the St. Lawrence
Saint-Ferréol-les-Neiges and the inland foothill climbs — where the route turned away from the river toward the Laurentians
Rang Saint-Achillée, Saint-Léon and Saint-Elzéar — the sustained inland climbing sections on the longer routes, with ramps reported up to 17%

logistics.lodging_strategy: Hotels and resorts available in Beaupré and nearby Mont Sainte-Anne area, including ski lodges repurposed for summer/fall events; not verified as currently applicable given no confirmed edition since 2019.

history.origin_story: First run June 22-23, 2013, founded by Sébastien Roy under the 'Gran Fondo Éco' umbrella, explicitly modeled on Gran Fondo New York, with Olympic alpine skier Mélanie Turgeon as ambassador. Luc Beaudet was event director by 2014. The event ran annually through 2019 (dates drifted from June to July across editions), with a planned July 19, 2020 eighth season cancelled by Quebec's province-wide summer event shutdown during COVID-19. It never relaunched.
notable_moments: 2014-2019: Annual editions, dates drifting from June into July
2020: Planned July 19 edition cancelled by Quebec's COVID-19 public-event shutdown
2021-2026: No confirmed edition; the former granfondomsa.com domain was newly re-registered in July 2025, consistent with the original operation having lapsed

biased_opinion.verdict: Apparently Defunct — No Confirmed Edition Since 2019
biased_opinion.summary: There is no evidence GranFondo Mont Sainte-Anne has run since its last confirmed edition, July 14, 2019. It was a real, regionally solid Côte-de-Beaupré cyclosportive founded in 2013 by Sébastien Roy (explicitly modeled on Gran Fondo New York), running annually through 2019 with distances from 46 to 161 km and documented climbing to 2,550 m on the longest course. A planned eighth-season edition was scheduled for July 19, 2020 — a date that fell within Quebec's province-wide COVID-19 summer event shutdown, though no event-specific cancellation notice for this race was found, only the general provincial order. No organizer announcement, registration page, or independent result has surfaced for 2021-2026, and the event's former domain was newly re-registered by an unrelated party in 2025 — consistent with (though not proof of) the original operation having lapsed rather than merely paused.
strengths: Historically real Côte-de-Beaupré and Laurentian-foothill terrain, with documented climbing to 2,550 m on the 160 km Alto course and ramps up to 17%
A credible regional organizer pedigree (Sébastien Roy's Gran Fondo Éco umbrella) and a recognizable local ambassador (Olympic skier Mélanie Turgeon) at launch
Four distances (46/80/121/161 km) historically gave a genuine range of entry points
weaknesses: No confirmed edition since July 14, 2019 — this is not a currently bookable event

The planned 2020 return fell within Quebec's province-wide COVID-19 event shutdown and was never rescheduled, with six years of silence since (no event-specific cancellation notice was found, only the general provincial order)

The promotional '2,000 riders / 600 finishers' claim in older coverage is not independently verified — the best-documented field figure is roughly 400 riders in 2014, against an original ~750-rider promotional capacity
This database's prior 130.4 km/1,800 m vitals don't match any documented historical route distance — treat them as unverified, not canonical
biased_opinion.bottom_line: Treat this as defunct, not bookable. If you want the Côte-de-Beaupré/Mont Sainte-Anne cycling experience this event once offered, look for a currently confirmed Quebec road sportive instead.

final_verdict.one_liner: A real Côte-de-Beaupré fondo, silent since 2019 — its planned 2020 return fell within Quebec's COVID shutdown and was never revived.
final_verdict.should_you_race: No — there is no current edition to register for, and no evidence of a 2021-2026 return. If a revival is ever announced, revisit; until then, choose an active Quebec road sportive instead.
final_verdict.alternatives: For a currently active Quebec road sportive, check the FQSC (Fédération québécoise des sports cyclistes) event calendar.

videos.title: Pré-fondo Mont-Sainte-Anne
videos.channel: La Télévision d'Ici Côte-de-Beaupré Île d'Orléans
videos.description:
Plusieurs amateurs de vélo étaient présents le 1er juin dernier au Château Mont-Sainte-Anne pour participer au pré fondo. 
Le rallye cycliste est en fait un prétexte pour se dégourdir comme il le faut les jambes avant le gran fondo prévu pour les 22 et 23 juin. 
Il y avait deux gangs, on en suivi une et je peux vous dire que ce sont de véritables athlètes que nous avons filmé. 
Ils sont partis du Château-Mont-Sainte-Anne pour se rendre en un temps record par l'avenue Royale à Château-Richer et s'aventurer dans la montée continuelle et sinueuse du rang St-Achillée. 
Ils ont gravi la pente jusqu'en haut sans presqu'être essoufflés. 
De là, un petit ravitaillement et ils sont repartis vers le Mont-Sainte-Anne et le rang St-Antoine.
Pour ceux qui n'y sont jamais allé, je crois que les images parlent d'elles-mêmes
Une petite randonnée à vélo de plusieurs kilomètres.

videos.title: Une horde de cyclistes attendus pour le Gran Fondo Mont-Sainte-Anne
videos.channel: La Télévision d'Ici Côte-de-Beaupré Île d'Orléans
videos.description:
La Côte-de-Beaupré sera l'hôte d'un rassemblement cycliste d'envergure internationale à l'occasion du Gran Fondo Mont-Sainte-Anne qui regroupera quelques 2000 cyclistes les 22 et 23 juin prochain. 
Présenté sur deux jours et sur trois parcours différents, ce qui signifie en italien « grande randonnée » est un événement accessible à tous qui se déroulera pour la première fois sur les routes panoramiques et les montées sinueuses qui caractérisent la Côte-de-Beaupré. 
L'ambassadrice du Gran Fondo, Mélanie Turgeon, championne du monde de ski alpin, olympienne et ancienne membre du club du Mont-Sainte-Anne a accepté de donner un coup de pédale pour faire connaître le nouvel arrivé de la série des Gran Fondo. 
Pour connaître tous les détails, le journaliste Maxime Rioux s'est rendu à la boutique Hype Ski Vélo à Québec pour rencontrer  l'athlète retraitée et porte-parole de l'événement, Mélanie Turgeon, le directeur marketing et le directeur logistique du Gran Fondo Mont-Sainte-Anne, Francis Kimpton et Luc Beaudet.

videos.title: Maximumsports.tv 196 - Gran Fondo, Mont-Ste-Anne - 130711
videos.description: Lorsque plus de 600 passionnés de cyclisme se retrouvent pour une grande première, les résultats peuvent surprendre. La première édition du Gran Fondo tenu au Mont Ste-Anne et sur la Côte-de-Beaupré aura connu un beau succès laissant place à un avenir prometteur.

eligibility.notes: CONFIRMED via codex gpt-5.6-sol foreground research, editorial wave 8. No registration, results, organizer announcement, or confirmed edition found for 2020-2026. Annual editions ran 2013-2019 (dates drifted June to July: June 22-23 2013, July 13 2014, July 19 2015, July 3 2016, July 16 2017, July 15 2018, July 14 2019). A planned eighth season was scheduled for July 19, 2020, a date that fell within Quebec's province-wide summer public-event shutdown order, though no event-specific cancellation notice was found — only the general provincial order. It was never rescheduled. The event's former domain (granfondomsa.com) was newly re-registered by an unrelated party in July 2025 — consistent with, though not proof of, the original operation having lapsed. Corrected date field from a stale '2026: September TBD' placeholder to reflect the defunct status. Vitals (130.4 km/1,800 m) are geographically plausible for the Côte-de-Beaupré region but do not match any documented standard historical route distance (nearest were 121 km medium / 160-161 km long) — flagged via catalog_flags rather than silently rewritten, since no better-sourced figure exists. SOL REVIEW CORRECTION (2026-07-24): a follow-up sol adversarial review found the initial draft's 'defunct since 2020, cancelled by COVID' framing stated more certainty than the evidence supports (the provincial order is general, not an event-specific cancellation) and that the domain re-registration claim lacked a citation — both corrected above; status retained as 'defunct' given six-plus years of total silence, but causation language softened.
